message:
feat(e2e): type-safe benchmark taxonomy, inline bundle emission, and stricter fixture validation

Replace free-form string placement/hardware identifiers with enum types
(PlacementProfileKind, PlacementAvailability, HardwareOsFamily, etc.)
and structured HardwareClassIdFields. Add --emit-inline-bundle CLI flag
for hot-path profiles that emits a tagged single-line JSON bundle on
stderr. Strengthen campaign validation with SHA256 format checks, path
uniqueness, taxonomy consistency, and required profile/class assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -279,7 +279,9 @@ ad hoc shell history:
 - Seed policy: fixed root seed `42`
 - Retry policy: harness-instrumented busy retries (`10_000`, `1ms` base, `250ms` cap)
 - Placement profiles: `baseline_unpinned`, `recommended_pinned`, `adversarial_cross_node`
+  with taxonomy kinds `baseline`, `recommended_pinned`, `adversarial_topology`
 - Hardware classes: `linux_x86_64_any`, `linux_x86_64_many_core_numa`
+  with identifier fields `os_family`, `cpu_arch`, `topology_class`
 
 Artifact names for canonical runs must include the source revision and the
 `.beads/issues.jsonl` hash so results stay mechanically diffable over time. The

@@ -68,14 +68,22 @@
   "hardware_classes": [
     {
       "id": "linux_x86_64_any",
-      "arch": "x86_64",
+      "id_fields": {
+        "os_family": "linux",
+        "cpu_arch": "x86_64",
+        "topology_class": "any"
+      },
       "min_logical_cores": 4,
       "min_numa_nodes": null,
       "description": "Generic Linux x86_64 host suitable for baseline scheduler-default comparisons."
     },
     {
       "id": "linux_x86_64_many_core_numa",
-      "arch": "x86_64",
+      "id_fields": {
+        "os_family": "linux",
+        "cpu_arch": "x86_64",
+        "topology_class": "many_core_numa"
+      },
       "min_logical_cores": 16,
       "min_numa_nodes": 2,
       "description": "Many-core Linux x86_64 host with NUMA or CCD boundaries suitable for pinned and adversarial topology evidence."
